name: Randomized IPF D+Q pilot supported tolerability but not efficacy
summary: The randomized IPF trial improves safety and feasibility coverage while limiting the earlier open-label functional interpretation because exploratory functional measures did not appear meaningfully different between D+Q and placebo.
statement: In a 12-participant randomized IPF pilot, all participants completed scheduled dosing and assessments, there were no D+Q-related serious adverse events, but non-serious adverse events were more common in the D+Q arm and exploratory frailty, pulmonary, or physical-function measures did not appear meaningfully different between groups.
population_or_model: Participants with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis aged over 50 years
time_horizon: Three weeks of intermittent D+Q or placebo
quantitative_note: The abstract reports 108 of 108 doses and 60 of 60 planned assessments completed; non-serious adverse events were 65 in the D+Q arm versus 22 in placebo.
caveats: The trial was intentionally underpowered for efficacy.
Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis is a disease-specific context, not healthy aging.
No D+Q-related serious adverse events in a 12-person pilot does not settle long-term safety.
